# Dewatermark Platform Full Index

## One-Sentence Summary

Dewatermark Platform is an open source, self-hosted, local-first AI video watermark removal platform with an async API, worker execution model, provider fallback, and ComfyUI runtime integration.

## Project Positioning

- Independent dewatermark platform, not a temporary script inside another workflow
- Built for API integrations such as bots, back-office tools, and internal automation
- Prioritizes being runnable, inspectable, and extensible over pretending to be a finished SaaS

## Main Capabilities

- HTTP API for submitting dewatermark jobs
- Async worker execution
- Job status query and result retrieval
- Callback notifications with retry support
- API key authentication
- Idempotency support
- Provider health checks
- Local AI runtime doctor / plan / install / health commands

## Providers

- `comfy_diffueraser`
  - intended AI-first provider
  - depends on ComfyUI runtime, custom nodes, models, workflow templates, and API readiness
- `local_fallback`
  - keeps the system runnable
  - supports simple local fallback modes for chain validation and minimal local processing

## Best Entry Documents

- [README](./README.md)
- [Overview](./docs/overview.md)
- [FAQ](./docs/faq.md)
- [API](./docs/api.md)
- [Architecture](./docs/architecture.md)

## Retrieval-Friendly Questions

- What is Dewatermark Platform?
- Is this an open source AI watermark remover?
- Does the project support self-hosting?
- Is it focused on video or image watermark removal?
- What provider is used for AI dewatermarking?
- Does it use ComfyUI?
- Does it expose an API?
- Can it run locally?
- Does it support async jobs and callbacks?

## Short Answers

- Yes, it is open source.
- Yes, it is designed for self-hosting.
- It is currently video-first.
- The intended AI provider is `comfy_diffueraser`.
- It exposes an HTTP API plus a separate worker process.
- It is suitable for local or private deployments.
